claims.claim_text: Optogenetic studies in mice indicate that activating fast-spiking or parvalbumin interneurons can amplify or generate cortical gamma rhythms, while gamma-cycle timing can shape sensory-response precision.
claims.normalized_claim: optogenetic studies in mice indicate that activating fast spiking or parvalbumin interneurons can amplify or generate cortical gamma rhythms while gamma cycle timing can shape sensory response precision

claim_atoms.text: Optogenetic studies in mice indicate that activating fast-spiking or parvalbumin interneurons can amplify or generate cortical gamma rhythms
gamma-cycle timing can shape sensory-response precision

claim_scope.computational: gamma-frequency circuit dynamics and sensory-response timing
claim_scope.conceptual: fast-spiking/parvalbumin interneuron control of gamma rhythms

passages.text: light-driven activation of fast-spiking interneurons at varied frequencies (8–200 Hz) selectively amplifies gamma oscillations.
the timing of a sensory input relative to a gamma cycle determined the amplitude and precision of evoked responses.

citation_contexts.independence_basis: Companion Nature studies share Feng Zhang and Karl Deisseroth; conservatively treated as one collaboration family.
citation_contexts.notes: Direct evidence in mouse barrel cortex for gamma amplification and gamma-phase-dependent response precision.

passages.text: driving these interneurons (even by means of non-rhythmic principal cell activity) is sufficient to generate emergent gamma-frequency rhythmicity.

citation_contexts.independence_basis: Companion Nature studies share Feng Zhang and Karl Deisseroth; conservatively treated as one collaboration family.
citation_contexts.notes: Companion-study support for the gamma-rhythm atom; shared authors mean it is not counted as an independent group. This source is not attributed to the timing/precision atom.

traceability.rationale: Both DOI-linked citations have verified abstract passages and their eligible atom mappings cover a1 and a2.
traceability.recommended_fix: Preserve passage locators and atom-specific attribution.

robustness.rationale: All atoms are supported, but the companion studies share authors and are conservatively counted as one collaboration family.
failure_modes: no independent collaboration supports every atom
robustness.recommended_fix: Add direct support from an author-disjoint collaboration and document its independence basis.

uncertainty_calibration.rationale: The sentence uses 'studies indicate' and 'can', matching direct but not per-atom replicated evidence.
uncertainty_calibration.recommended_fix: Keep the qualified wording unless per-atom replication is added.

source_integrity.rationale: Both relied-upon sources are primary, DOI-linked, direction-matched, passage-verified, and integrity-checked.
source_integrity.recommended_fix: Re-run publisher and integrity-registry checks when refreshing the review.

transferability_scope_control.rationale: The sentence explicitly limits the evidence to optogenetic studies in mice and both citation scopes match.
transferability_scope_control.recommended_fix: Retain the organism and method qualifiers.

claims.claim_text: Gebru and colleagues proposed datasheets for datasets: a standard document recording a dataset's motivation, composition, collection process, recommended uses, and maintenance - modelled on the datasheets that accompany electronic components.
claims.normalized_claim: gebru and colleagues proposed datasheets for datasets a standard document recording a dataset s motivation composition collection process recommended uses and maintenance modelled on the datasheets that accompany electronic components

claim_atoms.text: Gebru and colleagues proposed datasheets for datasets
a standard document recording a dataset's motivation, composition, collection process, recommended uses
modelled on the datasheets that accompany electronic components

passages.text: To address this gap, we propose datasheets for datasets.
every dataset be accompanied with a datasheet that documents its motivation, composition, collection process, recommended uses, and so on.
the process of creating, distributing, and maintaining a dataset
every component, no matter how simple or complex, is accompanied with a datasheet

citation_contexts.independence_basis: Single originating source; replication independence is not claimed.
citation_contexts.notes: Originating paper directly supports all elements of the historical method-attribution claim.

knowledge_edges.subject: datasheets for datasets
knowledge_edges.object: dataset motivation, composition, collection, uses, and maintenance

traceability.rationale: The DOI-linked originating source has verified passages and covers all four atoms.
traceability.recommended_fix: Keep the atom-to-passage record with the primary source.

robustness.rationale: This is a historical method-attribution claim and the originating primary paper covers every atom.
robustness.recommended_fix: Retain the originating paper as direct support.

uncertainty_calibration.rationale: The sentence reports what the paper proposed and does not claim downstream effectiveness.
uncertainty_calibration.recommended_fix: Keep the claim limited to the proposal's documented contents.

source_integrity.rationale: The relied-upon source is primary, DOI-linked, passage-verified, direction-matched, and integrity-checked.
source_integrity.recommended_fix: Re-run integrity checks on review refresh.

transferability_scope_control.rationale: The claim remains within the paper's machine-learning dataset-documentation scope.
transferability_scope_control.recommended_fix: Preserve the method and domain boundary.

claims.claim_text: Mitchell and colleagues added model cards: short reports of a model's intended use, its limitations, and - crucially - its performance broken out by subgroup and context rather than as a single headline number.
claims.normalized_claim: mitchell and colleagues added model cards short reports of a model s intended use its limitations and crucially its performance broken out by subgroup and context rather than as a single headline number

claim_atoms.text: Mitchell and colleagues added model cards
its performance broken out by subgroup and context rather than as a single headline number

passages.text: we propose a framework that we call model cards, to encourage such transparent model reporting.
In order to clarify the intended use cases of machine learning models
what machine learning systems can and cannot do, the types of errors they make
model performance that contain quantitative evaluation results to be broken down by individual cultural, demographic, or phenotypic groups

citation_contexts.independence_basis: Single originating source; replication independence is not claimed.
citation_contexts.notes: Originating paper directly supports the model-card contents and disaggregated reporting atoms.

knowledge_edges.subject: model cards
knowledge_edges.object: intended use, limitations, and disaggregated performance

traceability.rationale: The DOI-linked originating source has verified passages and covers all four atoms.
traceability.recommended_fix: Keep the atom-to-passage record with the primary source.

robustness.rationale: This is a historical method-attribution claim and the originating primary paper covers every atom.
failure_modes: treating proposal contents as an empirical effectiveness claim
robustness.recommended_fix: Retain the claim type and originating source.

uncertainty_calibration.rationale: The sentence accurately reports proposed model-card contents without claiming that model cards eliminate harms.

source_integrity.rationale: The relied-upon source is primary, DOI-linked, passage-verified, direction-matched, and integrity-checked.
source_integrity.recommended_fix: Re-run integrity checks on review refresh.

transferability_scope_control.rationale: The claim stays within the trained-model reporting scope of the proposal.

claims.claim_text: Bender and Friedman proposed data statements for language technology to document represented populations and dataset provenance; they argued that this practice could reduce bias and improve the precision of claims about generalization.
claims.normalized_claim: bender and friedman proposed data statements for language technology to document represented populations and dataset provenance they argued that this practice could reduce bias and improve the precision of claims about generalization

claim_atoms.text: Bender and Friedman proposed data statements for language technology
improve the precision of claims about generalization

passages.text: we propose data statements as a design solution and professional practice for natural language processing technologists
making it clearer which populations are and are not represented
passages.locator: Section 3, Why Does NLP Need Data Statements?

passages.text: For datasets built out of existing datasets, the data statements for the source datasets should be included as an appendix.
data statements will help alleviate issues related to exclusion and bias in language technology
lead to better precision in claims about how natural language processing research can generalize

citation_contexts.independence_basis: Single originating source; replication independence is not claimed.
citation_contexts.notes: The sentence explicitly attributes the proposal and its bias/generalization rationale to the originating paper.

traceability.rationale: The DOI-linked originating source has verified passages and covers all five atoms.
traceability.recommended_fix: Keep the atom-to-passage record with the primary source.

robustness.rationale: This is a historical method-and-argument attribution claim; the originating paper covers every atom.
robustness.recommended_fix: Retain explicit attribution for the paper's proposed consequences.

uncertainty_calibration.rationale: The proposal is stated as historical fact and the consequence is explicitly framed as the authors' argument.
failure_modes: restating the argument as an established causal effect

source_integrity.rationale: The relied-upon source is primary, DOI-linked, passage-verified, direction-matched, and integrity-checked.
source_integrity.recommended_fix: Re-run integrity checks on review refresh.

transferability_scope_control.rationale: The sentence is bounded to language technology and the source scope matches.
failure_modes: generalizing the proposal's rationale to all datasets
